title: Microsoft unveils Project Solara AI, a chip-to-cloud platform built to power a new generation of 'agent-first' enterprise devices hardware designed to run AI agents instead of traditional apps
summary: Microsoft has unveiled Project Solara, an AI-first enterprise device platform combining hardware and cloud services for adaptive interfaces and AI agents. The system leverages Qualcomm and MediaTek hardware, along with Azure-hosted agents. Reference designs include wearables and desktop devices.
insight: Enabling businesses to automate and transform with AI-powered enterprise devices.

title: Trump signs AI executive order seeking 30-day government access to frontier models before release voluntary framework will include classified benchmark to determine which models qualify
summary: President Donald Trump signed an executive order requiring AI companies to give the US government early access to their most advanced models. The move aims to establish a voluntary framework for evaluating AI capabilities. The framework will include a classified benchmark to determine which models qualify.
insight: US government seeks control over high-stakes AI development and deployment.

title: Noctua's first-ever AIO features a silenced Asetek Emma V2 pump and NF-A12/14 fans 240mm NL-LC1 starts at around $250, could cost $325 for 420mm cooler
summary: Noctua enters the AIO market with its NL-LC1 cooler, featuring a customized Asetek Emma V2 pump and silent fans. It starts at around $250 for the 240mm variant, with a potential price of $325 for the 420mm model. The SecuFirm2+ mounting system ensures secure installation.
insight: Asetek Emma V2 pump brings high performance and noise reduction

title: Tom's Hardware Unfiltered: Computex 2026, Day 2 Interviews, roundtables, and the first day at the Nanggang Exhibition Center
summary: Computex 2026 Day 2: Tom's Hardware team explores Nanggang Exhibition Center, attending interviews and roundtables, and showcasing new technology.
insight: Industry leaders converge on Taipei for groundbreaking tech announcements.

title: 32GB of DDR5 now costs $375 minimum AI shortage continues to squeeze PC building
summary: 32GB of DDR5 RAM has reached a minimum price of $374.97, indicating a significant shortage.
insight: DDR5 RAM shortage severely impacts PC building budgets and performance.

title: Corsair shows off gaming mouse with dedicated Stream Deck launch button wireless mouse also gets almost 50 hours of 8K battery life
summary: Corsair unveiled the Nightsword v2 Wireless SD gaming mouse with a dedicated Stream Deck launch button, offering users enhanced control and convenience during gaming sessions. The device provides virtually seamless integration with popular streaming software. It boasts an impressive battery life of almost 50 hours at 8K resolution.
insight: Streamers and gamers get instant access to Stream Deck functions

title: Intel's 5.7 GHz Xeon 6377P features 12 P-cores and a desktop-class LGA1700 socket unusual server CPU prioritizes clock speed over core count
summary: Intel's Xeon 6377P features a 5.7 GHz boost clock, 12 P-cores, and ECC support, prioritizing clock speed over core count for entry-level enterprise workloads.
insight: Prioritizing clock speed over core count for single-threaded performance matters

title: AMD says new EXPO Ultra Low Latency DDR5 memory should be 'effectively the same price' as current kits feature will work on existing chipsets, but will require new DIMMs
summary: AMD's new EXPO Ultra Low Latency DDR5 memory offers improved performance with existing chipsets through new DIMMs, with a price comparable to current kits.
insight: Enhanced performance at no significant cost increase expected.

title: Github Copilot customers report up to 100-fold price hikes AI sticker shock bites as Microsoft switches to usage-based pricing
summary: Github Copilot customers face significant price hikes due to Microsoft's switch to usage-based pricing, with reported increases of up to 100-fold.
insight: Businesses must adapt to unpredictable AI costs and pricing models.

title: The rise of local agentic computing faces a brutal reality: rising DRAM prices RTX Spark, Gorgon Halo chips subject to 63% DRAM contract price hike this quarter
summary: DRAM contract prices are forecast to climb 58-63% this quarter, affecting local agentic computing.
insight: Increased DRAM costs threaten affordability of advanced computing technologies.

title: Astera Labs showcases 320-lane PCIe 6.0 switch for vendor-agnostic scaling in data centers up to 80 accelerators can be scaled up using PCIe alone
summary: Astera Labs has showcased the Scorpio X-Series 320-lane PCIe 6.0 switch, enabling vendor-agnostic scale-up in data centers. This technology promises to support up to 80 accelerators using PCIe alone. The switch is suitable for AI infrastructure and disaggregated data centers.
insight: Breaks vendor lock-in for AI infrastructure scalability

title: Cherry XTRFY launches first 8K ultra-wideband gaming keyboard featuring more compact 70-percent layout
summary: Cherry XTRFY launched the first 8K ultra-wideband wireless gaming keyboard with a 70-percent compact layout, low-profile switches, and a gasket mount design at Computex 2026, offering gamers a new level of wireless performance and portability.
insight: Revolutionizes wireless gaming with ultra-wideband technology and compact design

title: Microsoft announces Majorana 2 quantum computing chip claims a practical machine will come in 2029
summary: Microsoft's Majorana 2 quantum computing chip has shifted to lead-based materials. This decision accelerates their roadmap, with a practical quantum machine expected in 2029. The chip aims to overcome previous challenges.
insight: Practical quantum computing within reach by 2029, transforming industries worldwide

title: Asus unveils its first Wi-Fi 8 router ROG Rapture GT-BN98 Pro offers up to 2x real-world throughput uplift over Wi-Fi 7
summary: Asus has unveiled its first Wi-Fi 8 router, the ROG Rapture GT-BN98 Pro, which offers up to 2x real-world throughput uplift over Wi-Fi 7, aimed at improving real-world performance.
insight: Enhanced real-world performance with increased Wi-Fi 8 throughput.

title: Intel s next-gen LGA1954 socket will support Nova Lake, Razor Lake, and beyond finally an Intel socket that outlives its CPUs
summary: Intel's next-gen LGA1954 socket will support various CPU generations, outliving them. This socket will support Nova Lake and Razor Lake CPUs, and possibly beyond. The new socket aims to reduce waste and upgrade costs.
insight: Long-lasting socket reduces waste and upgrade costs for users

title: Microsoft debuts Surface RTX Spark Dev Box Nvidia-powered mini-PC helps devs get ready for an agentic Windows
summary: Microsoft unveiled the Surface RTX Spark Dev Box, a mini-PC for developers to create AI applications. This system is designed to help developers prepare for the upcoming agentic Windows. It is powered by Nvidia, which provides the necessary hardware for AI development.
insight: Accelerating AI innovation with powerful Nvidia-powered development tools.

title: MSI unveils latest set of WiFi 7 gaming routers touting ultra-fast speeds flagship RadiXBE19000 model comes with a built-in SSD slot for 'NAS Lite' experience and wireless speeds up to 19 Gbps
summary: MSI has unveiled three WiFi 7 gaming routers, including the flagship BE19000 model. This model features an SSD slot, eight antennas, and a unique design. It offers ultra-fast wireless speeds of up to 19 Gbps.
insight: Enhanced gaming performance with ultra-fast WiFi 7 speeds

title: Computex 2026 Day One Wrap-Up: Arm makes a bold play for Windows PCs, PCIe 6.0 SSDs are coming, Asus embraces black and gold for ROG 20th
summary: At Computex 2026, Arm announced a significant push into Windows PCs, marking a bold shift in the market. Meanwhile, PCIe 6.0 SSDs are set to revolutionize storage, and Asus is celebrating its 20th ROG anniversary with a new black and gold aesthetic.
insight: Arm challenges Intel's dominance in Windows PC market

title: Cooler Master shows off new MWE Gold V4 Power supplies and GPU Shield adapter per-pin monitoring can dynamically scale down power to stop cables melting
summary: Cooler Master showcases new power supplies and GPU Shield adapter at Computex. The MWE Gold V4 Power supplies and GPU Shield adapter feature per-pin monitoring, dynamically scaling down power to prevent overheating and cable damage.
insight: Dynamic power scaling prevents GPU cable damage and overheating issues.

title: Mainland Chinese exhibitors reportedly locked out of Computex 2026, as Taiwan entry permits stall parties complain applications left pending or hit with last-minute documentation requests
summary: Mainland Chinese exhibitors are being locked out of Computex 2026 due to stalled entry permits, with many applications left pending or rejected at the last minute. This has caused frustration among the companies, who had planned to showcase their products at the event. Taiwan's entry permit process for mainland Chinese companies is reportedly causing the delays.
insight: Taiwan's entry permit process hinders cross-strait tech collaboration efforts.

title: Intel reportedly no longer working on 6-core Nova Lake mobile SKU, alleges new rumor Wildcat Lake Refresh to become focus for next-gen budget markets instead
summary: Intel is reportedly not developing a 6-core Nova Lake mobile SKU, prioritizing Wildcat Lake instead. The 6-core part was targeted at budget markets. Nova Lake is still expected to be announced at the end of the year.
insight: Intel shifts focus from budget 6-core to Wildcat Lake Refresh.

title: AMD is considering a potential Ryzen 5 9600X3D company says six-core Zen 5 X3D chip 'maybe something we look at doing... later this year'
summary: AMD is considering a 6-core Ryzen 5 9600X3D chip with Zen 5 architecture. This would be a departure from their current X3D offerings, which are 8-core. The company deems 6-core X3D chips impractical for broad market adoption.
insight: Potential shift to 6-core X3D chips with Zen 5 architecture emerging.

title: Cooler Master shows off new HAF II 500 chassis, aluminum fans, and new air coolers New V8 Cooler, Masterfan A, and updated Silencio 600 and HAF chassis add to an already comprehensive product stack
summary: Cooler Master showcased new products at Computex 2026, including the HAF II 500 chassis, aluminum fans, and air coolers. The company expanded its product stack with the Masterfan A, Silencio 600, and V8 Cooler. The updated lineup aims to meet growing demands for custom cooling solutions.
insight: Cooler Master strengthens its position in the custom cooling market.

title: Phison shows PCIe 6.0 X3 SSD controller with 28 GB/s of bandwidth and 6.8 million IOPS, supports 2 petabytes per drive also new power-sipping E37T SSDs for PCIe 5.0 systems consume a mere 4.5W
summary: Phison unveiled its PCIe 6.0 X3 SSD controller with impressive performance claims, boasting up to 28 GB/s sequential throughput and 6.8 million IOPS in random workloads. The X3 controller also supports storage capacities of up to 2 petabytes per drive. Additionally, Phison showcased power-efficient E37T SSDs for PCIe 5.0 systems that consume a mere 4.5W.
insight: Groundbreaking SSD performance boosts data-intensive applications and storage capacities.

title: Comet Q USB-C KVM device is made for smartphones, tablets, and laptops (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Comet Q is a KVM device for remote control of mobile devices via a USB-C cable and web browser, with a touchscreen LCD for control and information display.
insight: Enables seamless remote control of mobile devices with web-based interface simplicity.

title: Reolink OMVI triple-lens AI security cameras combine 4K PTZ camera with dual-lens system with fixed 180 panoramic view
summary: Reolink OMVI triple-lens AI security cameras combine a 4K PTZ camera with a fixed dual-lens system offering a 180 panoramic view, designed to eliminate blind spots and enhance surveillance capabilities.
insight: Enhanced surveillance capabilities with seamless panoramic and zoom views.

title: Morse Micro MM8108-M20 high-power Wi-Fi HaLow module delivers up to 28.5 dBm Tx output power
summary: The Morse Micro MM8108-M20 is a high-power Wi-Fi HaLow module offering up to 28.5 dBm transmit output power. It operates in the 850-950 MHz range and supports various modulation schemes for increased data rates. This module is suitable for applications requiring longer range and higher power transmissions.
insight: Regulatory variations enable higher power transmissions, increasing range in specific regions.

title: AI hallucinations in embedded are, yes, a thing
summary: Bob Martin, a Microchip Technology expert, shares his skeptical journey with vibe coding, highlighting AI hallucinations in embedded systems as a concern.
insight: AI-generated code in embedded systems can lead to unexpected behavior.

title: Sensors are essential to robots, as Computex made clear
summary: A robot barista at Computex efficiently served 200 cups of coffee per hour, showcasing sensors' importance in robotics.
insight: Precision sensors enable high-speed, high-quality robotic performance in various applications.

title: GTA V cheating platform Atlas Menu hacked, exposing emails and encrypted passwords
summary: The cheating platform Atlas Menu has been hacked, affecting tens of thousands of gamers. The breach exposed users' emails and encrypted passwords. The incident was reported by Have I Been Pwned (HIBP).
insight: Gamers' sensitive information is compromised due to cheating platform hack.

title: Google ordered to let publishers in UK opt out of AI search summaries
summary: Google must introduce tools to let UK publishers opt out of AI-powered Search features.
insight: Publishers' content rights and control are a major regulatory concern.

title: ILL's full trailer is packed with zombie baby-punching, eye-gouging, and ultra-realistic body horror
summary: ILL's full trailer showcases intense zombie action and ultra-realistic body horror, building hype after concept teasers were first released in 2021.
insight: Sony's ILL trailer unleashes graphic content for horror fans worldwide.

title: Intel's upcoming LGA 1954 socket reportedly supports Nova Lake, Razor Lake, and possibly beyond
summary: Intel's LGA 1954 socket may support multiple processor generations, including Nova Lake and Razor Lake.
insight: Flexibility in motherboard designs could enhance future upgrade options.

title: Windows 11 may soon let you uninstall the AI models Microsoft keeps installing on your PC
summary: Windows 11 may allow users to uninstall AI models installed by Microsoft. The feature is part of the experimental preview build 26300.8553. This build includes various improvements and bug fixes.
insight: Users may have control over AI models on their PCs

title: Intel launches Xeon 6+ Clearwater Forest with 288 E-cores on 18A node, teases Diamond Rapids for 2027
summary: Intel releases Xeon 6+ Clearwater Forest with up to 288 E-cores and 576 MB L3 cache.
insight: Massive core count boosts high-performance computing capabilities significantly.

title: Proton Mail now lets you send email from your Gmail address
summary: Proton Mail now allows Gmail users to send and receive emails using their Gmail address within the Proton Mail platform, making it easier for people to switch from Gmail to Proton Mail.
insight: Easier transition to secure email service for Gmail users.

title: Asus ROG Ally X20 goes OLED, but the mandatory AR glasses kill the bundle
summary: The Asus ROG Ally X20 now features a 7.4-inch OLED display, offering improved visuals. The upgrade replaces the previous 7-inch LCD screen. The OLED panel reaches 1,400 nits of brightness.
insight: Upscale visuals with OLED display upgrade in gaming handheld.

title: Gabe Newell says Steam doesn't pressure developers on pricing, emails suggest otherwise
summary: Steam CEO Gabe Newell denies pressuring developers on pricing, but emails contradict him.
insight: Valve's pricing policies may impact small game developers' revenue.

title: A startup is using AI to find lithium deposits directly beneath Europe's battery factories
summary: A startup uses AI and historical data to identify lithium deposits near European battery factories, spanning 1.5 million acres around Salzgitter and Wroclaw, where major battery manufacturing facilities are located.
insight: Closing the loop on local battery production with nearby lithium reserves.

title: Nvidia brings back the RTX 3060 12GB for $350 as GPU market gets weird again
summary: Nvidia is reportedly restarting production of the RTX 3060 12GB, originally discontinued in 2024. The move comes amid huge disruption to graphics card supply due to RAMageddon. The card is set to be released again at $350.
insight: GPU market volatility sparks Nvidia to revive a popular model.

title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman in test of who is liable for AI harms
summary: Florida has sued OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, over AI safety concerns.
insight: AI safety accountability is being tested at the state level nationwide.

title: China's military obtained Nvidia chips despite US export controls, report claims
summary: China's military obtained Nvidia chips despite US export controls. A business-intelligence firm found repeated procurement attempts by People's Liberation Army units. Nvidia hardware was bought through Chinese procurement documents.
insight: US export controls may be circumvented by Chinese procurement tactics.

title: Hackers tricked Meta's AI chatbot into handing over Instagram accounts, including Obama's
summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI chatbot to hijack Instagram accounts, including Obama's. The issue started after the chatbot's introduction in March. Account takeovers involved high-profile figures.
insight: Vulnerability in AI support system compromises high-profile social media security

title: Dashlane users locked out after brute-force attacks target password manager accounts
summary: Dashlane users experience login issues and account suspensions due to brute-force attacks.
insight: Massive security breach impacts sensitive password data and user trust.
